title: Causal rolling degradation slope features
summary: Recent F1 degradation research models tyre pace as a latent state with pit-stop resets, not just static tyre age.
rationale: Recent F1 degradation research models tyre pace as a latent state with pit-stop resets, not just static tyre age. The current best has cumulative degradation ratios but little causal momentum of degradation within the current stint.
implementation_hint: Sort by Year/Race/Driver/LapNumber. Within each stint, create lag-only rolling/EWMA features for LapTime_Delta, Pace_Deviation_From_Median, Cumulative_Degradation, first differences, 3-lap slopes, and acceleration terms; reset at Stint or PitStop changes. Avoid centered or future windows.
expected_effect: May improve AUC by catching the lap just before the tyre cliff, where next-lap pit probability rises sharply.
risk: If row order or race grouping is noisy, rolling features can add variance; validation must ensure no future leakage.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
